element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Foundational color matching mechanic introduced at lowest difficulty
Second color introduced simultaneously with red for basic pattern recognition
Third color completes the basic matching triad
Core mechanic: rotation and placement of heterogeneous pairs for matching
Gravity causes cascading matches after initial elimination; chains add bonus points and opponent capsules in multiplayer
Virus population scales with difficulty; requires faster decision-making and planning
Three capsule fall speeds available; faster speeds compress time-to-decision
Chain eliminations send capsules to opponent field; first to 3 wins match
Playfield narrow at top creates spatial pressure; overflow triggers loss

typical_relief_triggers: Chain reaction cascades provide scoring bursts
typical_tension_triggers: Capsules fill bottle toward narrow neck
Limited rotation space at top of playfield
Opponent sends cascading capsules in multiplayer
Difficulty spikes between level increments increase virus density
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Continuous low-to-moderate tension with periodic relief through eliminations; tension builds gradually as bottle fills; complete level clears provide narrative breathing room via cutscenes every 5 levels

difficulty_curve.early_game: Levels 0-5: Low virus density, wide open playfield, ample space for experimentation and learning rotation mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Levels 6-15: Virus density increases progressively; playfield constraint becomes real factor; chain reactions become essential for efficiency
difficulty_curve.late_game: Levels 16-20: High virus density requires forward planning; limited space for error; cutscenes at Levels 5, 10, 15, 20 provide paced narrative relief
notable_spikes: Level 0→1 jump increases initial virus count
Mid-game density scaling (Levels 6-10) requires shift from reactive to tactical play
Levels 16-20 approach maximum constraint; board state planning becomes mandatory

notable_level_patterns.description: Viruses spawn in fixed starting positions and do not move until matched; creates predictable spatial layout per level allowing planning-ahead strategies
Player selects starting level (0-20) which directly determines virus population; higher levels pack more viruses requiring tighter play
After a match, unsupported capsule halves fall and trigger new matches; enables chain reactions and secondary scoring opportunities
After every 5th level completion (5, 10, 15, 20), a cutscene plays showing virus characters in new location with flying object; breaks mechanical rhythm with story beats
The playfield's narrow neck at top represents the loss condition; as density increases, this space becomes the critical resource
